rulings.Case Title: SANTA ROSA MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al VS. DAVID MAXWELL-JOLLY DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Stay Previously Ordered By This Court And To Vacate Or Continue The September 29, 2015 Hearing Date
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 16, RESPONDENTS DAVID MAXWELL-JOLLY DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES AND TOBY DOUGLAS' Motion To Enforce The Stay Previously Ordered By This Court And To Vacate Or Continue The September 29, 2015 Hearing Date. The court orders the stay lifted and continues the petition for writ of mandate hearing to October 27, 2015. The opposition and reply papers are to be filed in accordance with CCP 1005. Armstrong v. Exceptional Child Ctr., Inc., 135 S.Ct. 1378 (2015) and Cal. Hosp. Ass'n v. Maxwell-Jolly (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 559, 569, indicate that adjudication of petitioner's Medicare/Medi-Cal claim cannot occur in federal court and petitioner's remedy is to seek a writ per CCP 1085. There is little chance that the 9th Circuit will reverse Judge Conti's order. Respondent has an onerous task in showing that Judge Conti abused his discretion by dismissing the federal action without prejudice. See Westlands Water Dist. v. United States, 100 F.3d 94, 96 (9th Cir. 1996). Any res judicata or collateral estoppel argument that respondent may make is dubious because the federal court never addressed the underlying merits of the dispute. Lastly, any further delay will prejudice petitioner who is seeking reimbursement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: AARON HASHIM et al VS. JOHN CHIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT BETTY YEE's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT The motion to strike the Third Amended Complaint is granted. On December 4, 2014, the Court issued an order sustaining Defendant's demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint. The order states: "Qualified immunity shields Mr. Chiang from liability as an individual. 'Government officials performing discretionary functions generally are shielded from liability for civil damages insofar as their conduct does not violate clearly established statutory or constitutional rights of which a reasonable person would have known.' (Harlow v. Fitzgerald (1982) 457 U.S. 800, 818.) The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") pleads that Mr. Chiang complied with the provisions of the UPL. (SAC, pars. 15, 20.) The Ninth Circuit upheld the new escheatment statutory scheme as Constitutional on its face. (Taylor v. Westly (9th Cir. 2008) 525 F.3d 1288, 1289 ("Taylor III").) Because the Complaint does not allege conduct outside the parameters of the escheatment statutes, a reasonable person would not have known that his conduct would violate Constitutional rights. Although the section 1983 action against Mr. Chiang individually fails, Plaintiff may be able to amend the SAC to state a cause of action against the State directly to the extent it seeks damages equal to the amount of the property held in trust only or an injunction. (See Taylor v. Westly (9th Cir. 2005) 402 F.3d 924, 932-35 ("Taylor I"); see also Suever v. Connell (9th Cir. 2009) 579 F.3d 1047, 1059, 'Our case law states that the Eleventh Amendment does not bar claims by plaintiffs for the return of their own property under the UPL, because such claims are not for 'damages' against the State.'¿ The demurrer to the first cause of action for Declaratory Relief is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. There may be a basis for a declaratory relief cause of action, but its contours would have to be consistent with the ruling on the demurrers to the other causes of action." In his Third Amended Complaint, Plaintiff has not alleged any cause of action against the State directly. Further, the basis for declaratory relief is not consistent with the ruling on demurrer, therefore Defendant's motion to strike the Third Amended Complaint is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: AVA KNOX VS. JP MORGAN CHASE & CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. Defendant did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff as a matter of law. See Martinez v. Bank of America Nat. Trust & Sav. Ass'n (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 883, 892-893. Until the unlawful detainer judgment was obtained and satisfied, defendant lacked the ability to control the conditions on the premises. rulings.Case Title: SHANON LOFTUS VS. SEAN MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date Or To Reopen Discovery After New Trial Date Set; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SHANON LOFTUS' Motion To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date Or To Reopen Discovery After New Trial Date Set. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion of Plaintiff Shanon Loftus to Re-open Discovery is GRANTED. Upon the motion of any party, accompanied by a declaration showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at informal resolution of the issues presented therein, the Court may order that discovery be reopened after a new trial date has been set. (Code of Civil Procedure §2024.050.) The Court finds that there is a necessity for the Plaintiff to engage in discovery and the inaction of prior counsel should not prejudice her. Plaintiff, through new counsel has shown diligence in presenting this motion. Permitting discovery to be re-opened will not interfere with the trial calendar and no prejudice will be suffered by Defendant. Defendant cites no authority for the supposition that Plaintiff's motion is pre-mature because no trial date has been set. The Court has been unable to locate any authority indicating that it is an abuse of discretion to order discovery re-opened based upon a prospective trial date. It is ORDERED that discovery is re-opened and all discovery deadlines will run from the new trial date when it is set. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CLARK et al VS. SEGWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT SEGWAY LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Segway, Inc.s' Demurrer to 1st Amended Complaint as to Plaintiff's cause of action for fraud and breach of implied warranty is sustained. Plaintiffs are not persuasive in asserting that either defendant committed fraud. Plaintiffs' pleadings do not offer " 'facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.' " (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645, citing Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) Plaintiffs also fail to "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written", which is required for sustaining a claim of fraud against a corporate defendant. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) Leave to amend is granted if Plaintiffs can in good faith plead the specifics required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CLARK et al VS. SEGWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT CHARLES KEES, CHARLES KEES DBA SEGWAY SAN FRANCISCO BAY'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. Defendant Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay Motion to Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint For Monetary Damages as to punitive damages is granted. Plaintiffs' "conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement" to support their claim for punitive damages. (Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) General allegations that Defendant Segway, Inc. knowingly concealed the dangers of its product and did not provide a long enough training video, and that Defendant Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay's acted in a "despicable" manner by saying that the Segway was "100 percent safe" do not meet threshold requirements for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

Defendant Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay's Demurrer to 1st Amended Complaint as to Plaintiff's cause of action for fraud is sustained. Plaintiffs are not persuasive in asserting that either defendant committed fraud. Plaintiffs' pleadings do not offer " 'facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.' " (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645, citing Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) Plaintiffs also fail to "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written", which is required for sustaining a claim of fraud against a corporate defendant. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) Plaintiffs have not indicated how they can cure their inadequate pleadings. Leave to amend is granted if Plaintiffs can in good faith plead the specifics required. rulings.Case Title: ED FRAUENHEIM VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Notice And Subpoena, And For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Percepta Llcs Person(S) Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Motion To Quash Deposition Notice And Subpoena, And For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Percepta LLC'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable. Defendant, Ford Company's Motion to Quash Deposition Notice and subpoena and for a protective order prohibiting deposition of Percepta LLC's most knowledgeable is denied. There is inadequate showing that the requested discovery is unreasonable, oppressive or intended to harass the Defendant. To the contrary, the deposition sought is of a witness who allegedly was directly involved in the actions about which Plaintiff complains. As to Plaintiff's alleged failure to provide Defendant with a copy of the Florida subpoena, such failure can easily be corrected and, in any case, is not a basis for issuing the protective order sought by Defendant. The court awards a sanction of $1,000.00 to plaintiff and against Ford and its attorneys payable by September 30, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MAGNATE FUND #2, LLC VS. MARCIA GIUSTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT MARCIA GIUSTI, MARTIN CUTLER, GEORGE AKWO, GA LAW GROUP'S Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Motion To Strike. Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted. Plaintiffs do not establish the second prong of their claim for malicious prosecution. Even if the evidence supporting Defendants' underlying cause of action was ultimately admissible, that does not mean that the action lacked probable cause. Even if the court was not persuaded by Defendants' claim for collusion/conspiracy, Defendants have persuasively shown that initiating and maintaining the underlying suit was not baseless. That Defendants made unpersuasive arguments in their pleadings and were unable to admit certain dispositive evidence does not on their own establish a lack of probable cause in initiating or maintaining an action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT VIRACON, INC.'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MONZERRAT PRESTEGUI MENDOZA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT,A GOVERNMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 11, 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, SARA SHENKEN-RICH, RYAN YOUNG, DANIEL BRIDGES, GINI DOLD'S Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint. Continued to September 23, 2015, on the Court's own motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. (302/EG)
